titlestmt.titleproper: Peoria, Ill. Plymouth Congregational Church records, 1889-1899.
titlestmt.author: Zachary Bodnar

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 21, 2026 at 14:21

did.unittitle: Peoria, Ill. Plymouth Congregational Church records, 1889-1899.
did.abstract: In 1869, the First Congregational Church of Peoria established a Sunday School, named the Plymouth Mission. The Plymouth Congregational Church was organized out of the Plymouth Mission on June 2, 1889, with Rev. Charles Clark Harrah as its first minister. In 1896, the church constructed a new brick edifice on the same site. The church dissolved in 1919. This collection contains a single church record volume which documents both vital member records and the minutes of meetings.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is unrestricted and open to the public.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are subject to U.S. Copyright Law. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine the copyright status of collection items and to secure any permissions necessary for their reproduction and publication. Requests for permission to publish material must be discussed with the archivist or librarian.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], in the Peoria, Ill. Plymouth Congregational Church records, 1889-1899, RG5586. The Congregational Library & Archives, Boston, MA.

acqinfo.head: Acquisition Information
acqinfo.p: Materials were donated to the Congregational Library & Archives by the Illinois Conference of the United Church of Christ in December 2024; accession number 2024-46.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed by Zachary Bodnar, April 2026, according to the rules of Describing Archives: A Content Standard.

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: In 1869, the First Congregational Church of Peoria established a Sunday School, named the Plymouth Mission, at the corner of Fourth and Spencer Streets. The school was successful and by the end of 1888, talk had begun on the establishment of a church. With supporting funds for a church secured, Rev. Charles Clark Harrah was called to become minister of the church. The Plymouth Congregational Church was formally organized on June 2, 1889.

The early years of the church saw multiple ministers called to serve. Rev. David Brainard Spencer was called in 1890, Rev. Stafford William Meeks in 1894, Rev. Frank G. Smith in 1898, and John William Nelson in 1990. In 1896, the church constructed a new brick edifice on the same site as the old site.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains a single church record volume which documents the church's membership, includes baptismal records for infants and adults, and records minutes for the first ten years of the church's existence. A small manuscript celebrating the fifth anniversary of the church is inserted into the volume.

bibliography.head: Bibliography
bibliography.p: Bateman, Newton, Paul Selby, and David McCulloch. Historical Encyclopedia of Illinois and History of Peoria County. Chicago and Peoria: Munsell Publishing Company, 1902. https://genealogytrails.com/ill/peoria/church/EarlyChurches.htm.
